title: Exploring Magnets — True or False Quiz
instructions_for_student: Read each statement carefully and click True or False. Think back to what you observed in the experiments before answering. After all four statements, you'll see your final score with a review of each answer.

quizData.title: True or False?
quizData.subtitle: Read each statement carefully and click True or False. Think back to what you observed in the experiments!

statements.statement: A magnet can be broken into pieces to obtain a single pole.
statements.explanation: Correct! Even the tiniest broken piece always has both a North and a South pole.
statements.reason: This is FALSE. Breaking a magnet always produces new pairs of poles — you can never isolate a single pole.

statements.statement: Similar poles of a magnet repel each other.
statements.explanation: Correct! Like poles (N–N or S–S) always push each other away — repulsion!
statements.reason: This is TRUE. Like poles repel; unlike poles attract — observed clearly in Activity 4.5.

statements.statement: Iron filings mostly stick in the middle of a bar magnet when brought near it.
statements.explanation: Correct! The magnetic force is strongest at the poles (ends), so filings cluster there.
statements.reason: This is FALSE. Iron filings cluster at the two poles (ends), not in the middle — as Activity 4.2 showed.

statements.statement: A freely suspended bar magnet always aligns with the north-south direction.
statements.explanation: Correct! Earth behaves like a giant magnet, pulling the suspended magnet to align N–S.
statements.reason: This is TRUE. Earth's magnetic field causes any freely suspended magnet to align north-south — the basis of a compass.

schema.description: Configuration schema for TrueFalseQuiz — an interactive one-at-a-time True/False quiz component. Themed with Poppins font and the Singularity design system palette (purple #533086, orange #FC9145, blue #4A4DC9).

celebrateOnComplete.description: Trigger a particle-burst celebration when a correct answer is selected.

quizData.description: Full quiz content. Override to customise for any chapter or topic.

title.description: Main heading displayed at the top of the quiz.

subtitle.description: Instruction text shown below the heading.

statements.description: Array of statement objects shown one at a time.

id.description: Unique numeric ID for the statement.

statement.description: The declarative sentence the student evaluates.

answer.description: true if the statement is factually correct, false if it is incorrect.

explanation.description: Short positive confirmation shown when the student answers correctly.

reason.description: Brief corrective explanation shown when the student answers incorrectly. Should include the correct answer.

agent_readme.overview: TrueFalseQuiz shows statements one at a time. Students click True or False; the result is shown immediately with a colour-coded feedback box and a one-line explanation. After all statements, a score screen displays an animated score, star rating, per-statement summary, and a Try Again button.

design_system.no_external_deps: Uses only React + lucide-react. No react-dom, no portals.

quick_reference.answer_field: boolean — true means the statement is True, false means it is False
quick_reference.navigation: one statement at a time; Next button appears after each answer

tool_call_examples.description: Custom quiz for a different chapter

quizData.title: Light & Shadows — True or False?
quizData.subtitle: Think carefully before answering each statement.

statements.statement: Transparent objects allow all light to pass through them.
statements.explanation: Correct! Transparent materials like clear glass let light pass through completely.
statements.reason: This is TRUE. Transparent objects transmit light without scattering.

statements.statement: A shadow is formed on the side facing the light source.
statements.explanation: Correct! Shadows form on the opposite side from the light source.
statements.reason: This is FALSE. Shadows form on the side away from the light, not the side facing it.

decision_tree.chapter_review: Use default parameters — 4 statements load automatically.
decision_tree.more_statements: Extend the statements array; each needs id, statement, answer, explanation, reason.
decision_tree.fewer_statements: Reduce the array; the progress dots and score adapt automatically.
